entries.value: Ryan prefers concise answers with high signal and low filler.
The Local LLM page should stay out of Home and be surfaced as its own product area.
The launcher should feel more like a calm native chat product with more room for transcript and composer.
MemPalace is being evaluated as a challenger memory backend and should not replace the baseline without direct comparison evidence.
Home should stay calm and chat-first, while heavier runtime, routing, and recovery detail belongs in App Mgmt or dedicated local-model surfaces.
Route and voice evidence should stay visible to users, but in plain language that does not re-clutter Home.
Workspaces should read as intentional user-facing contexts with clearer purpose, recurring context, and collaboration cues.
The chat interface should feel more like iOS or Messenger: softer bubbles, calmer composer rhythm, and more breathing room for the transcript.
On this machine, qwen3:8b is currently the safer challenger to keep pushing first because it is faster and less reasoning-noisy than qwen3:30b in the controlled guppy_local runs.
